notes: HOTFIX for v1.6.49 regression. The v1.6.49 named-constant refactor used replace_all on quoted string literals to swap inline keys for named constants, which ALSO matched the literals on the right-hand side of my newly-added const declarations. Result: six self-referencing const declarations (e.g. `const BURNDOWN_KEY = BURNDOWN_KEY;`). JavaScript TDZ rule: reading a const inside its own initializer throws ReferenceError. Since the first self-reference was at file scope (BURNDOWN_KEY block), execution halted there and EVERYTHING declared afterward never ran — BD IIFE, AUDIT IIFE, OPS_TABS late additions, boot-restore IIFE, post-boot UI wiring. App painted but no event handlers attached, so all buttons appeared locked with no visual feedback. node --check passed because self-reference is a RUNTIME error, not a syntax error. Fix: restored the six declarations to their proper string-literal initializers — BURNDOWN_KEY, HANDOFF_NOTES_KEY, RACK_VIEWER_LAST_KEY (file scope), GE_LAST_RACK_KEY + GE_LAST_FAULT_KEY (GE IIFE), ACTIVE_AUDIT_KEY (AUDIT IIFE). Also reverted the HTML inline oninput at the handoff-notes textarea back to the literal 'phantom_handoff_notes' — inline event handlers in browsers cannot resolve script-top-level const bindings (they only see globals on window). The const value is still used by the JS-side read site. Builds on v1.6.50.
